openGraph.title: BMW M60/M62 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
openGraph.description: The M60/M62 V8 is characterful and robust – but timing chain guides are a known risk, backed by UK specialist data. Compare real replacement quotes today.

twitter.title: BMW M60/M62 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
twitter.description: The M60/M62 V8 is characterful and robust – but timing chain guides are a known risk, backed by UK specialist data. Compare real replacement quotes today.

@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the M60/M62

mainEntity.name: How much does an M60/M62 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M60/M62 typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement and exact variant. Fitting adds £800–£1,700 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M60/M62 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: We rate the M60/M62 at 3.5 stars out of 5, based on a 90% confidence level from specialist reports and market data [ESTIMATED]. It is a robust V8, but timing chain guide wear is the single most significant long-term risk.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M60/M62 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timing chain guide tensioner fails, allowing chain slap and eventual guide breakage, typically between 80,000–120,000 miles. Early M60 engines also suffered Nikasil bore wear on poor fuel – resolved by 1996.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the M60/M62?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M60/M62 powered the E31 840Ci, E32/E38 7 Series, E34/E39 5 Series (540i), and E53 X5 4.4i. All are rear‑wheel‑drive V8 cruisers, with the X5 adding all‑wheel drive.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M60/M62 the same as Land Rover’s version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes – the 4.4‑litre M62B44 was also fitted to the Land Rover L322 Range Rover (2002–2005). The engine is mechanically identical, though Land Rover used its own ECU calibration and engine mounts.

hero.h1: BMW M60/M62 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M60/M62 is BMW's first modern V8 family — early Nikasil lining issues resolved, but timing chain guides remain a known weakness. Backed by proprietary enquiry data [BMW-VERIFIED].

rows.value: Also fitted to Land Rover L322 Range Rover

verdictRating.confidence: 90% — based on cross-referenced specialist reports and UK market data.

rows.score: 12 (Mixed failure modes: some cheap fixes (gaskets, sensors), but timing chain guide replacement is labour-intensive)
16 (Well-supported by BMW specialists and aftermarket, though some VANOS components are becoming scarce)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M60/M62 V8 is a characterful, robust engine — but timing chain guides fail without warning past 100,000 miles — buy only with documented guide replacement.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts who appreciate a smooth, torquey V8 and are willing to maintain it properly.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented timing chain guide or VANOS service history above 80,000 miles.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover L322 Range Rover (M62B44) → [Land Rover M62 page link].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M60/M62 typically takes 12–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across BMW and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.
